steps.title: Substitute $3x=30$ into the target expression
steps.content: The expression $3x-12$ contains $3x$, and the given equation states $3x=30$, so replace $3x$ with $30$ to get $30-12$.

steps.title: Compute the target value
steps.content: So $3x-12 = 30-12 = 18$. That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. Subtracting $12$ from each side of the given equation yields $3x-12=30-12$, or $3x-12=18$. Therefore, the value of $3x-12$ is $18$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $x-12$, not $3x-12$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $x+12$, not $3x-12$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $3x+12$, not $3x-12$.
